reviews.review_description:
I thoroughly enjoyed this game, and I believe it was a wonderful experience. it does have it's fair share of bugs, but those are being worked on and are expected. I would recommend this game to those who like western games, and i do believe it is a fun experience aside from the bugs.

Spoilers!!

This game has four weapons, the revolver, shotgun, sniper, and dual pistols. there are also two side weapons, being the flints and grenades. I thoroughly enjoyed the sniper missions, and the dual revolver ones are really good too. I personally did not prefer the shotgun missions, and i found the normal revolver ones were ok, more missions to be expected. the side weapons only had two or so missions each, i found i had a blast with the grenade but found the flint to be lacking. There are some thirty missions give or take, and each of them have their pros and cons. a common thing i found i enjoyed was the enemy rush ones and the ones where good aim were required, though there were a few other types, including two quick time ones. Quite a few missions i found to be challenging, and provided a good amount of thinking and planning to get through. overall, 4/5 due to bugs and a few nitpicks but otherwise a great game and a fun experience.

reviews.review_title: A solid but simple western shooter game
reviews.review_description:
Outlaws Assassin is a solid but simple VR western shooter that puts you in the boots of a gunslinger taking down enemies in fast-paced, arcade-style encounters. It focuses heavily on gunplay, reflexes, and immersion, rather than deep storytelling or complex systems. 

The core strength of the game is its shooting. Quick draw mechanics, aiming, and reactions feel natural, making firefights engaging.

It’s easy to pick up and play. You can jump in for short sessions.

The western setting, combined with physical movement (aiming, dodging, reloading), gives a strong sense of presence.

The gameplay loop can feel repetitive after a while. There’s not much progression or variety.

Don’t expect a large-scale experience—this is more of a focused shooter than a full adventure.

reviews.review_title: SMART AND FUN VR WESTERN SHOOTER
reviews.review_description: A clever mix of stealth and shooting in a Wild West setting. Fun mechanics and varied missions make it enjoyable for both action and strategy players.

developer_response.body: Dear User, We are so glad you enjoyed.

reviews.review_description: Graphics and mechanics are horrible, I take cover and still take damage, one guy does like 1000 damage, should be free, not worth the money.

developer_response.body:
----------Update May 2026-------
We heard your feedback and , Please update the game and replay all levels 
--------------------------------------
In all games in the world enemies in games always have different difficulty levels and damage values, that’s basic game design. Some enemies are meant to be stronger so players can’t just ignore positioning. Also, taking cover can work in different ways sometimes with a crouch button, and sometimes physically in VR by moving your body behind objects in the real world. If you’re still taking damage, it usually means you’re not fully behind cover. Unfortunately, that’s not an issue with the game mechanics.

reviews.review_title: Solid game you get what you ask for
reviews.review_description:
Great game very fun, took me less then 20 minutes to finish, it's short but has "decent" replay value, I honestly enjoyed it a lot and found out I'm a much better shot then I thought, it has a few quick draw duels and a good puzzle aspect to some missions, it's very short however and replaying would get very repetitive if you go for 3 stars every mission

Overall it's the best western vr game I've ever played so far, only glitches I found was my hitbox falling through a gap in the wall then having to restart the game and (not as bad) the menu screen not opening at all

I'm only saying 4/5 stars because of how short it is, I think if they release more levels and make it longer or even give missions a weapon choice instead of forcing one + freedom of movement it'd be perfect (open world would be 6/5 but that's asking a lot) if any of the developers see this you guys cooked HARD with this I'm gonna replay it right now

developer_response.body:
----------Update May 2026-------
We heard your feedback and , Please update the game and replay all levels and share your feedback again, also you can join our discord channel to share more details.
https://discord.gg/Be4fR3Pn
--------------------------------------

Thank you so much for the detailed feedback, really appreciate you taking the time to write this. We’ve been dealing with a few things lately but honestly we’re super happy to hear you enjoyed the game. Totally fair points about the length, replay value and the bugs you mentioned, we’re definitely taking notes on all of that. Hearing that you had fun and are already replaying it means a lot to us. Thanks again for the support, it really motivates the team.

reviews.review_description:
This game is made simple but fun especially for those who entered the world of VR recently.
Graphics aren't that detailed and high but the experience is actually fun and engaging .
Due to its high comfortability the game is actually playable for anybody anywhere.

This game drops you into fast-paced mini action scenes where you need to assess each situation, then act quickly—eliminating outlaws and saving the girl, the bank, or both. The progression system is solid, offering the ability to unlock and purchase new weapons as you go. One standout feature is the clever use of a mirror to peek around corners and locate enemies—adding a fun layer of strategy and immersion. It's a smart, satisfying take on classic Western shootouts!

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot! Glad you enjoyed the pacing and the mirror mechanic—it was a fun feature to design. Appreciate the feedback! 🤠🎯
